perturbation.original_text: The Company will bill you monthly for the Services. You authorize the Company to instruct Prime Trust or any escrow agent used by Company to deduct such fees, debts and any other amounts liabilities incurred under this Service Agreement, prior to releasing any amounts due to you or to any other person (including another escrow agent) from escrow. Amounts which remain unpaid for 30 days are subject to a finance charge of 1.5% per month on any outstanding balance, or the maximum permitted by law, whichever is lower, plus all expenses of collection and may result in immediate termination of Service. You shall be responsible for all taxes associated with Services other than U.S. taxes based on the Company's net income.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will bill you monthly for the Services. You authorize the Company to instruct Prime Trust or any escrow agent used by Company to deduct such fees, debts and any other amounts liabilities incurred under this Service Agreement, prior to releasing any amounts due to you or to any other person (including another escrow agent) from escrow. You shall be responsible for all taxes associated with Services other than U.S. taxes based on the Company's net income.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the clause 'Amounts which remain unpaid for 30 days are subject to a finance charge of 1.5% per month on any outstanding balance, or the maximum permitted by law, whichever is lower, plus all expenses of collection and may result in immediate termination of Service.' creates uncertainty about late payment penalties. This omission contradicts the later section 4(ii) that addresses the Company's ability to collect unpaid reversal liabilities, including potentially applying finance charges if late payment penalties are not defined. This creates a contradiction because the original text clearly defines penalties while the revised text does not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Returns and Reversals a) Returns and Reversals User transactions debited from bank accounts via ACH are subject to returns (e.g., non-sufficient funds) and reversals from chargebacks (e.g., unauthorized activity) per the Electronic Fund Transfer Act (15 U.S.C. 1693 et seq. as may be amended), Regulation E, and NACHA guidelines (collectively, such returns and reversals are "Reversals"). The Company will work to protect Customer and the receiving Users from unwarranted Reversals; however, Customer acknowledges and agrees that: i) Customer is liable for all User Activity and Reversals associated with User Activity; ii) If Company's agent receives a Reversal, the Company may in its sole discretion charge Customer the full amount of the Reversal ("Reversed Payment") plus an additional $7 reversal fee ("Reversal Fee" and collectively the "Reversal Liability"); iii) The Company has sole discretion to determine who is at fault and liable for the Reversed Payment and Reversal Fee; iv) Customer authorizes the Company to take any of the following actions (in any particular order): (i) collect the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability from funds sent to your third party escrow account; (ii) debit your bank account in the amount of the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability; (iv) engage in collection efforts to recover the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability and/or (v) take legal action or any other action under this Service Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the clause detailing late payment penalties directly contradicts section 'Returns and Reversals', specifically regarding the company's ability to collect unpaid reversal liabilities and potentially apply finance charges. The original text provides a clear framework for penalties, while the revised text eliminates this, creating uncertainty and a potential conflict regarding the enforcement of penalties described in the Returns and Reversals section. This qualifies as a strong contradiction because the absence of defined penalties creates ambiguity about the conditions under which the company can impose charges related to reversals.

perturbation.original_text: iv) Customer authorizes the Company to take any of the following actions (in any particular order): (i) collect the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability from funds sent to your third party escrow account; (ii) debit your bank account in the amount of the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability; (iv) engage in collection efforts to recover the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability and/or (v) take legal action or any other action under this Service Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: iv) Customer authorizes the Company to take any of the following actions (in any particular order): (i) collect the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability from funds sent to your third party escrow account; (v) take legal action or any other action under this Service Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The clause '(ii) debit your bank account in the amount of the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability; (iv) engage in collection efforts to recover the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability and/or' has been removed. This omission introduces a contradiction because while the Company is authorized to collect unpaid liabilities via legal action, the ability to directly debit the customer's bank account is removed, creating a contradiction with section 2(b). Section 2b. states: 'You authorize the Company to instruct Prime Trust or any escrow agent used by Company to deduct such fees, debts and any other amounts liabilities incurred under this Service Agreement.' Thus, the Company has the authorization to extract the reversal liability, in section 2b, yet lacks the ability to do so in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Monthly Fees and Billing The Company will bill you monthly for the Services. You authorize the Company to instruct Prime Trust or any escrow agent used by Company to deduct such fees, debts and any other amounts liabilities incurred under this Service Agreement, prior to releasing any amounts due to you or to any other person (including another escrow agent) from escrow. Amounts which remain unpaid for 30 days are subject to a finance charge of 1.5% per month on any outstanding balance, or the maximum permitted by law, whichever is lower, plus all expenses of collection and may result in immediate termination of Service. You shall be responsible for all taxes associated with Services other than U.S. taxes based on the Company's net income.
perturbation.justification: The original text authorized the Company to debit the customer's bank account. The changed text removes this authorization. The contradicted text explicitly states, 'You authorize the Company to instruct Prime Trust or any escrow agent used by Company to deduct such fees, debts and any other amounts liabilities incurred under this Service Agreement'. The changed text now directly contradicts this statement, because the company is no longer explicitly authorized in the modified section to debit the bank account, even though the contradicted text states that it is. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to earlier termination as provided below, this Service Agreement is for the total duration of the Company's Offering (the "Initial Term") unless either party requests termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term. b) Additionally, either party may terminate this Service Agreement in the event: i) The other party's material breach that remains not cured and continues for a period of (A) in the case of a failure involving the payment of any undisputed amount due hereunder, 15 days and (B) in the case of any other failure, 30 days after the non performing party receives notice from the terminating party specifying such failure; ii) Any statement, representation or warranty of the other party is untrue or misleading in any material respect or omits material information; iii) The other party (A) voluntarily or involuntarily is subject to bankruptcy proceedings, (B) applies for or consents to the appointment of a receiver, trustee, custodian, sequestrator, or similar official, (C) makes a general assignment to creditors, (D) commences winding down or liquidation of its business affairs, (E) otherwise takes corporate action for the purpose of effecting any of the foregoing, or (F) ceases operating in the normal course of business; iv) If any change to, enactment of, or change in interpretation or enforcement of any law occurs that would have a material adverse effect upon a party's ability to perform its obligations under this Service Agreement or a party's costs/revenues with respect to the services under this Service Agreement; v) Upon direction to a party from any regulatory authority or National Automated Clearing House Association to cease or materially limit the exercise or performance of such party's rights or obligations under this Service Agreement; vi) If there shall have occurred a material adverse change in the financial condition of the other party; or vii) Upon a force majeure event that materially prevents or impedes a party from performing its obligations hereunder for a period of more than 10 business days.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to earlier termination as provided below, this Service Agreement is for the total duration of the Company's Offering (the "Initial Term") unless either party requests termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term. b) Additionally, either party may terminate this Service Agreement in the event: i) The other party's material breach that remains not cured and continues for a period of (A) in the case of a failure involving the payment of any undisputed amount due hereunder, 15 days; ii) Any statement, representation or warranty of the other party is untrue or misleading in any material respect or omits material information; iii) The other party (A) voluntarily or involuntarily is subject to bankruptcy proceedings, (B) applies for or consents to the appointment of a receiver, trustee, custodian, sequestrator, or similar official, (C) makes a general assignment to creditors, (D) commences winding down or liquidation of its business affairs, (E) otherwise takes corporate action for the purpose of effecting any of the foregoing, or (F) ceases operating in the normal course of business; iv) If any change to, enactment of, or change in interpretation or enforcement of any law occurs that would have a material adverse effect upon a party's ability to perform its obligations under this Service Agreement or a party's costs/revenues with respect to the services under this Service Agreement; v) Upon direction to a party from any regulatory authority or National Automated Clearing House Association to cease or materially limit the exercise or performance of such party's rights or obligations under this Service Agreement; vi) If there shall have occurred a material adverse change in the financial condition of the other party; or vii) Upon a force majeure event that materially prevents or impedes a party from performing its obligations hereunder for a period of more than 10 business days.
perturbation.explanation: The clause '(B) in the case of any other failure, 30 days after the non performing party receives notice from the terminating party specifying such failure' has been removed. This omission introduces a contradiction because it provides two conflicting timelines for curing a breach. Now, breaches involving payment of any undisputed amount due must be cured within 15 days, but all other failures lack a specific timeframe, creating uncertainty about when the non-breaching party can terminate the agreement in events where a breach does not involve payment, such as failure to make Services avaliable to Customer as described in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to earlier termination as provided below, this Service Agreement is for the total duration of the Company's Offering (the "Initial Term") unless either party requests termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the '30 days' cure period for breaches other than payment failure, creating a direct contradiction. The original text provides a termination condition based on a 30-day cure period for non-payment breaches. By removing the cure period, the changed text makes it uncertain when the non-breaching party can terminate the agreement for other breaches as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. This conflicts with the contradicted text: 'Subject to earlier termination as provided below' because the removal of a cure period directly affects when the agreement can be terminated. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
